programs.programName: Idaho Medicaid Aged, Blind, and Disabled (ABD) Program

verified.notes: Search results confirm age 65+ and $2,000 asset limit for single applicants in 2026. Monthly income limits not published in retrieved sources; only annual LTC limit of $2,901 mentioned in one non-gov source. Couple asset limit not found. Phone number not located in search results. Official state sources (healthandwelfare.idaho.gov) do not provide specific monthly income thresholds in retrieved excerpts.

verified.notes: SHIBA is a Medicare counseling and information program, not an assistance program with eligibility criteria. It provides free, unbiased Medicare benefits information and counseling to all Idahoans with Medicare regardless of age, income, or assets. No application process exists; seniors contact the helpline for information and counseling services. Program remains active as of April 2026.

programs.programName: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program (Title III-B & VII)

verified.notes: Program protects residents 60+ in long-term care facilities (nursing homes, assisted living). This is an ombudsman advocacy program, not a benefits program—no income or asset tests apply. Phone number not found in search results; contact through local Area Agency on Aging or Idaho Commission on Aging (aging.idaho.gov). Program is federally mandated under Older Americans Act Title VII, Sections 711/712, and operates statewide through State and Local Ombudsmen.
